metadata.author: Vision Capitalist / ULTRAMIND
metadata.purpose: Design retention-optimized onboarding sequences that create early breakthroughs, prevent dropout, activate community engagement, and build habit loops that transform customers into advocates through genuine transformation.

audit_log.check: Autonomy vs Automation: PASS (human-centered support escalation)
Quality Standards: PASS (measurable outcomes, not vanity metrics)
No Manipulation: PASS (genuine support, not guilt-based retention)
G.I.V.E. Framework: PASS (Gratify → Inspire → Validate → Enhance)
Convection Economics: PASS (breakthroughs create organic sharing)
Hologon Alignment: PASS (4-aspect balance: Clear, Focused, Harmony, Aligned)

pyramid_base_aspects.harmony: Aligned with transformation journey from Block 1
pyramid_base_aspects.aligned: Supports overall product promise and tier structure

hologon_mapping.zero_point_connection: Retention drives toward authentic transformation at soul/core

consciousness_profile.description: Onboarding maps to Levels 1-2 (ATTENTION → AWARENESS) with breakthrough pushing into Levels 3-4 (APPRECIATION → ACTIVATION). Retention maintains momentum through the full 6-level journey.

day_1_2.activation: Safety, security, "you're in the right place"
day_1_2.goal: Remove fear, establish trust, first small win

day_5_7.goal: Measurable breakthrough, logical proof of progress

convection_design.principle: Early breakthroughs create organic sharing; retention builds advocacy

viral_heat_indicators.indicator: Unsolicited Day 7 wins posted in community
Social media mentions with product tags
Community members helping new members (peer support)

layer1_directive.purpose: Define WHAT onboarding and retention principles are and WHY they matter

core_principles.explanation: First priority is creating a measurable quick win (Day 7-14), not consuming content. Information without application creates "shelf-ware." Breakthroughs create believers.
New customers need guidance more than modules. Day 1 should focus on "how to win" orientation and environment setup, not diving into Lesson 1.
Identify and eliminate barriers in first 48 hours. Technical issues, unclear next steps, overwhelming content, missing materials - all must be addressed immediately.
Isolated customers drop out. Connected customers persist. Day 1-3 must include community introduction, peer connection, and first social interaction.
Dropout signals are predictable (no login Day 3, no completion Week 1, silent in community). Proactive outreach prevents churn. Waiting for refund request = too late.
Self-serve → Community → Coaching → 1:1. Every customer has a path to more support if they struggle. Never let someone fail without offering help.

guardrails.no_content_dump: Never start with "Welcome to Module 1" - start with orientation
guardrails.no_isolation: Never leave new customer without community introduction by Day 3
guardrails.no_passive_dropout: Never let warning signals go unaddressed for 48+ hours
guardrails.no_guilt_retention: Never use shame, guilt, or fear to retain customers
guardrails.no_hidden_friction: Never assume frictionless - actively identify and remove barriers
guardrails.no_vanity_metrics: Track completion and transformation, not login counts

layer2_orchestration.purpose: Define HOW onboarding is designed and WHEN retention triggers fire

framework.name: 7-Day Breakthrough Sequence
framework.purpose: Engineer quick win in first 7-14 days through structured daily progression

day_1.name: Orientation + First Win
day_1.theme: Welcome + "How to Win" + First DO Task

component.name: Welcome Video
component.content: Personal welcome, "you made a great decision," here's what happens next

component.name: How to Win Guide
component.content: - The ONE thing that determines success - Common mistakes to avoid - Daily rhythm recommendation - How to get help if stuck

component.name: Environment Setup
component.content: - Account setup complete - App/platform orientation - Physical materials organized (if applicable) - Notification preferences set

component.name: First DO Task
component.content: One simple action that creates immediate micro-result. NOT consuming content. DOING something.
component.completion_ritual: Celebrate completion, share in community optional

component.name: Community Introduction
component.content: - Welcome thread or intro post template - Where to go for help - Who the community leaders are

day_1.warning_signal: No login within 24 hours of purchase

day_2.name: Friction Removal

component.name: Day 1 Check-in
component.content: How did first task go? Any issues?
component.format: AI check-in or automated email with reply prompt
component.action: If issues reported → immediate support response

component.name: Friction Audit
component.content: Quick self-assessment: - Technical: Can you access everything? - Time: Is your schedule blocked for this? - Environment: Is your space set up? - Support: Do you know where to get help?

component.name: Environment Optimization Task
component.content: One action to reduce friction identified in audit. Physical: organize space, gather materials Digital: set reminders, bookmark resources Social: tell someone about your commitment

component.name: Second DO Task
component.content: Continue momentum from Day 1, slightly deeper

day_2.warning_signal: Friction score above 6, or no Day 2 login

day_3_5.name: Breakthrough Engineering

day_3.content: First real "lesson" but tied to immediate practice
day_3.task: Apply methodology to ONE specific situation
day_3.community: First engagement beyond intro (comment, reaction, or question)

day_4.content: Refine Day 3 application, address common mistakes
day_4.task: Repeat with improvement based on feedback
day_4.community: Share progress or ask question in community

breakthrough_engineering.principle: Design for MEASURABLE result by Day 7-14

example.metric: Sleep quality score improvement (tracker or subjective)
First lead, first sale, or first outreach batch

example.target: One meaningful decision or breakthrough insight

day_3_5.warning_signal: No task completion for 2+ consecutive days

day_6_7.name: Breakthrough + Celebration
day_6_7.theme: First Measurable Result + Social Proof Generation

day_6.content: Apply full methodology for measurable outcome
day_6.support: Extra check-in available for those struggling

component.name: Celebration Ritual
component.content: - Acknowledge the win (even if small) - Connect to larger transformation - "This is proof you can do this"

component.name: Share Prompt
component.content: - Post win in community (template provided) - Optional: share on social media - Tag the program/brand

component.name: Identity Reinforcement
component.content: "You're not someone who just bought a course. You're someone who [achieved X] in 7 days. That's the kind of person who [achieves full transformation]."

breakthrough_validation.if_partial: Extend to Day 10-14 with extra support
breakthrough_validation.if_not_achieved: Intervention protocol: identify barrier, offer support escalation

integration_with_block_1.transformation_map_alignment: Day 1-7 = Phase 1 of transformation journey Breakthrough = Entry to Phase 2
integration_with_block_1.ldst_connection: Day 1-2: L = Learn (orientation) Day 3-5: D = Do (practice) Day 6-7: S = Share + T = Teach (community)

framework.name: Retention Trigger System
framework.purpose: Automated and human touchpoints that maintain engagement and prevent drift

examples.trigger: Morning motivation or task reminder (first 14 days)

type.name: Behavioral Triggers
type.description: Fires based on user action (or inaction)

trigger_frequency_guidelines.anti_pattern: Never more than 2 triggers per day (overwhelm creates unsubscribe)
Never pure "we miss you" without value offer

template.name: Weekly Check-in
template.subject: Week [X] Check-in: How's it going?
template.structure: 1. Acknowledge where they should be (Week X = Phase Y) 2. One quick tip for this phase 3. Community highlight (peer win to inspire) 4. Clear next action (not "catch up" - specific task) 5. Support offer (if struggling, here's help)

template.name: Milestone Celebration
template.subject: 🎉 You Did It: [Milestone Name]
template.structure: 1. Celebrate the accomplishment specifically 2. Connect to transformation progress 3. Share prompt (community or social) 4. Preview what's next 5. Reinforce identity ("This is who you're becoming")

template.name: Re-engagement (Gentle)
template.subject: Still with us? (Quick check-in)
template.structure: 1. No guilt or shame - just genuine check 2. Acknowledge life happens 3. One specific, easy re-entry action 4. Support offer (need help getting back on track?) 5. Community reminder (people are rooting for you)

framework.name: Dropout Prevention Framework
framework.purpose: Identify at-risk customers early and intervene before dropout

signal.name: Day 3 No Login
signal.description: No platform access 72 hours after purchase

signal.name: Week 1 No Completion
signal.description: Logged in but no task/module completion
signal.intervention: AI check-in: "What's blocking you?" + friction assessment

signal.name: Week 2 No Breakthrough
signal.description: Active but no measurable result by Day 14

signal.name: Community Silent
signal.description: Never posted or engaged in community
signal.intervention: Personal invite to specific thread + buddy pairing offer

signal.name: 14+ Days Inactive
signal.description: No login or activity for 2+ weeks
signal.intervention: Personal email from team member + easy re-entry path

signal.name: Support Request Unanswered
signal.description: Asked for help, didn't receive adequate response
signal.intervention: Immediate personal follow-up + escalation to senior support

step.name: AI Check-in
step.timing: If no response to Step 1 within 24 hours

step.name: Human Outreach
step.method: Personal email from team member (not automated)
step.content: Reference their specific situation, offer call or support session

options.option: Tier upgrade with payment plan (if VIP support needed)

step.name: Graceful Exit
step.method: If customer still wants out, make it easy
step.no_guilt: Thank them, honor guarantee, leave door open
step.data_capture: Exit survey to understand why (improves future retention)
step.alumni_path: Offer to stay in community even if refunding (builds goodwill)

barrier.symptoms: Can't access, login issues, platform confusion
barrier.solution: Immediate tech support, screenshare if needed
barrier.prevention: Better Day 1 orientation, clearer setup guides

barrier.symptoms: Too busy, life got in the way, overwhelmed
barrier.solution: Micro-task pathway, pause option, condensed track
barrier.prevention: Realistic time expectations in sales, daily tasks under 30 min

barrier.symptoms: Don't know what to do next, lost in content, unclear priority
barrier.solution: Personal guidance call, simplified roadmap, single next action
barrier.prevention: Better "How to Win" guide, clearer navigation, progress indicators

barrier.symptoms: Lost interest, doesn't see value, initial excitement faded
barrier.solution: Reconnect to WHY, peer success stories, quick win challenge
barrier.prevention: Stronger breakthrough engineering, community inspiration, identity reinforcement

barrier.symptoms: Not what they expected, wrong level, mismatch with needs
barrier.solution: Honest conversation about fit, possible tier change, graceful refund
barrier.prevention: Better sales qualification, clearer product description, "this is NOT for you if..."

framework.name: Community Activation Rituals
framework.purpose: Transform passive members into active community participants through structured rituals

ritual.name: Welcome Ritual

component.name: Introduction Template
component.prompt: "👋 Hi everyone! I'm [name] from [location]. I joined because [one sentence why]. My goal for the next 30 days is [specific goal]. One fun fact about me: [personal touch]"
component.friction_reduction: Template removes "what do I say?" barrier

component.name: Welcome Response
component.content: Personal welcome, acknowledge their goal, one tip or encouragement

component.name: Peer Welcomes
component.gamification: Optional: "Super Welcomer" badge for top welcomers

ritual.success_metric: 80%+ of new members post introduction within 48 hours

ritual.name: First Win Share
ritual.purpose: Create early momentum through visible progress

component.name: Win Thread
component.format: Dedicated thread or channel for wins
component.prompt: "🎉 FIRST WIN: What I did: [action] What happened: [result] How I feel: [emotion]"

ritual.convection_trigger: TRUE (wins create social proof for lurkers and outsiders)

ritual.name: Weekly Rhythms
ritual.purpose: Create predictable engagement patterns that become habit

monday_intentions.name: Monday Momentum
monday_intentions.prompt: "🎯 This week I'm focusing on: [one thing]"

wednesday_checkin.name: Midweek Check-in
wednesday_checkin.prompt: "📊 Progress check: How's your week going? (Green/Yellow/Red)"

friday_wins.name: Friday Wins
friday_wins.purpose: End week on positive note, create reflection habit

weekend_optional.name: Weekend Wisdom (Optional)
weekend_optional.content: Moderator shares insight, inspiration, or member spotlight

ritual.name: Milestone Celebrations
ritual.purpose: Mark progress publicly, reinforce identity, generate social proof

milestone.name: Full Program
milestone.celebration: Graduation ceremony (live or async) + alumni status + referral program invite

ritual.name: Accountability Structures
ritual.purpose: Create peer support systems that increase completion

buddy_system.description: Pair two members at similar stage
buddy_system.structure: Weekly check-in, shared goals, mutual support
buddy_system.matching: By timezone, goal type, or self-selection

pod_mastermind.description: Small group (4-6) with structured weekly call
pod_mastermind.structure: Hot seat rotation, goal setting, accountability

cohort_identity.description: Members who start together stay together
cohort_identity.structure: Cohort name, private channel, shared milestones
cohort_identity.impact: Cohort completion rates 40-60% higher than rolling enrollment

weekly_actions.action: Content prompt if activity is low

moderator_guidelines.tone: Encouraging, genuine, never condescending or fake

layer3_execution.purpose: Define tools, validation, and automation for onboarding/retention
layer3_execution.deterministic_tools:
7 All tools <100ms 100% reproducible outputs
<tool name="validate_7_day_sequence" performance="<50ms"> Verify 7-day sequence has all required components onboarding_sequence object valid (bool), missing_components (list), completeness_score (0-1) true
required_days = { 'day_1': ['welcome', 'how_to_win', 'first_do_task', 'community_intro'], 'day_2': ['check_in', 'friction_audit', 'barrier_removal'], 'day_3': ['core_methodology', 'practice_task', 'community_engagement'], 'day_4': ['practice', 'feedback'], 'day_5': ['breakthrough_prep', 'accountability_post'], 'day_6': ['breakthrough_execution'], 'day_7': ['celebration', 'share_prompt', 'identity_reinforcement', 'week_2_preview'] }
missing = [] total_components = 0 present_components = 0
for day, components in required_days.items(): total_components += len(components) if day not in onboarding_sequence: missing.append(f"Missing {day}") continue
for component in components: if component not in onboarding_sequence[day]: missing.append(f"{day}: missing {component}") else: present_components += 1
completeness_score = present_components / total_components valid = completeness_score >= 0.85 # Allow 15% flexibility
return valid, missing, completeness_score
<tool name="check_retention_triggers" performance="<30ms"> Validate retention trigger system completeness trigger_config object complete (bool), missing_triggers (list), coverage_score (0-1) true
required_triggers = { 'scheduled': ['daily_week_1', 'weekly_after_day_14', 'day_30', 'day_60', 'day_90'], 'behavioral': ['task_completion', 'streak_milestone', 'no_login_3_days', 'no_completion_7_days'], 'achievement': ['first_breakthrough', 'phase_complete', 'full_completion'], 'social': ['peer_recognition', 'cohort_milestone'] }
missing = [] total = 0 present = 0
for category, triggers in required_triggers.items(): total += len(triggers) if category not in trigger_config: missing.extend([f"{category}: {t}" for t in triggers]) continue
for trigger in triggers: if trigger in trigger_config[category]: present += 1 else: missing.append(f"{category}: {trigger}")
coverage_score = present / total complete = coverage_score >= 0.80
return complete, missing, coverage_score
<tool name="validate_dropout_prevention" performance="<25ms"> Ensure dropout prevention framework is complete prevention_config object valid (bool), gaps (list), readiness_score (0-1) true
required_signals = [ 'day_3_no_login', 'week_1_no_completion', 'week_2_no_breakthrough', 'community_silent', 'days_14_inactive', 'support_unanswered' ]
required_interventions = [ 'automated_reach', 'ai_checkin', 'human_outreach', 'escalation_offer', 'graceful_exit' ]
gaps = []
# Check signals for signal in required_signals: if signal not in prevention_config.get('signals', {}): gaps.append(f"Missing warning signal: {signal}")
# Check interventions for intervention in required_interventions: if intervention not in prevention_config.get('interventions', {}): gaps.append(f"Missing intervention: {intervention}")
# Check barrier classification barrier_types = ['technical', 'time', 'confusion', 'motivation', 'fit'] for barrier in barrier_types: if barrier not in prevention_config.get('barriers', {}): gaps.append(f"Missing barrier type: {barrier}")
total_required = len(required_signals) + len(required_interventions) + len(barrier_types) present = total_required - len(gaps) readiness_score = present / total_required
valid = readiness_score >= 0.85
return valid, gaps, readiness_score
<tool name="check_community_rituals" performance="<20ms"> Confirm community activation rituals are specified community_config object complete (bool), missing_rituals (list), activation_score (0-1) true
required_rituals = { 'welcome_ritual': ['intro_template', 'welcome_response', 'peer_welcomes'], 'first_win_share': ['win_thread', 'win_amplification'], 'weekly_rhythms': ['monday_intentions', 'friday_wins'], 'milestone_celebrations': ['week_1', 'phase_complete', 'day_30', 'graduation'] }
missing = [] total = 0 present = 0
for ritual, components in required_rituals.items(): total += len(components) if ritual not in community_config: missing.extend([f"{ritual}: {c}" for c in components]) continue
for component in components: if component in str(community_config[ritual]): present += 1 else: missing.append(f"{ritual}: {component}")
activation_score = present / total complete = activation_score >= 0.75
return complete, missing, activation_score
<tool name="validate_breakthrough_engineering" performance="<15ms"> Verify Week 1-2 contains measurable quick win design breakthrough_config object valid (bool), issues (list), breakthrough_score (0-1) true
required_elements = { 'metric_defined': 'What specific thing will improve?', 'target_set': 'What measurable improvement is expected?', 'measurement_method': 'How will they measure it?', 'timeline': 'By when (Day 7-14)?', 'baseline_captured': 'Is before state documented?', 'celebration_planned': 'How will win be celebrated?', 'share_mechanism': 'How will they share the win?' }
issues = [] present = 0
for element, description in required_elements.items(): if element in breakthrough_config and breakthrough_config[element]: present += 1 else: issues.append(f"Missing: {element} - {description}")
breakthrough_score = present / len(required_elements)
# Extra validation: timeline must be Day 7-14 if 'timeline' in breakthrough_config: day = breakthrough_config['timeline'] if isinstance(day, int) and (day < 7 or day > 14): issues.append(f"Timeline {day} outside Day 7-14 window") breakthrough_score -= 0.1
valid = breakthrough_score >= 0.80
return valid, issues, max(0, breakthrough_score)
<tool name="score_onboarding_friction" performance="<20ms"> Calculate friction score (lower is better) and identify barriers onboarding_design object friction_score (0-10), barriers (list), recommendations (list) true
friction_factors = [] recommendations = []
# Factor 1: Day 1 length day_1_minutes = onboarding_design.get('day_1_duration_minutes', 60) if day_1_minutes > 45: friction_factors.append(('day_1_too_long', (day_1_minutes - 45) / 15)) recommendations.append(f"Reduce Day 1 to under 45 minutes (currently {day_1_minutes})")
# Factor 2: Technical setup complexity setup_steps = onboarding_design.get('setup_steps', 5) if setup_steps > 5: friction_factors.append(('setup_complex', (setup_steps - 5) / 5)) recommendations.append(f"Simplify setup to 5 steps or fewer (currently {setup_steps})")
# Factor 3: First task difficulty first_task_minutes = onboarding_design.get('first_task_minutes', 15) if first_task_minutes > 15: friction_factors.append(('first_task_long', (first_task_minutes - 15) / 15)) recommendations.append(f"Shorten first task to under 15 minutes (currently {first_task_minutes})")
# Factor 4: Community introduction required? if not onboarding_design.get('community_intro_template', False): friction_factors.append(('no_intro_template', 0.5)) recommendations.append("Add introduction template to reduce 'what do I say?' friction")
# Factor 5: Help accessibility if not onboarding_design.get('help_visible_day_1', True): friction_factors.append(('help_hidden', 0.5)) recommendations.append("Make help/support visible on Day 1")
# Factor 6: Progress indicator if not onboarding_design.get('progress_indicator', False): friction_factors.append(('no_progress', 0.3)) recommendations.append("Add progress indicator so users know where they are")
# Calculate total friction score (0-10, lower is better) base_score = 2.0 # Minimum friction exists in any system friction_additions = sum([factor[1] for factor in friction_factors]) friction_score = min(10, base_score + friction_additions * 2)
barriers = [factor[0] for factor in friction_factors]
return round(friction_score, 1), barriers, recommendations
<tool name="check_escalation_path" performance="<10ms"> Validate support escalation path exists and is complete support_config object complete (bool), missing_levels (list), path_clarity (0-1) true
required_levels = [ ('self_serve', 'Knowledge base, FAQ, tutorials'), ('community', 'Peer support, community Q&A'), ('group_coaching', 'Scheduled coaching calls, office hours'), ('one_on_one', 'Direct support, VIP access') ]
missing = [] present = 0
for level, description in required_levels: if level in support_config and support_config[level].get('available', False): present += 1 else: missing.append(f"{level}: {description}")
path_clarity = present / len(required_levels)
# Bonus check: is escalation documented? if not support_config.get('escalation_documented', False): path_clarity -= 0.1 missing.append("Escalation path not documented for users")
complete = path_clarity >= 0.75
return complete, missing, max(0, path_clarity)

block.sync_point: Breakthrough should align with Phase 1 completion

block.integration: Onboarding varies by tier (Core = self-serve emphasis, VIP = white-glove)
block.sync_point: Escalation path matches tier support promises

block.receives: Community ritual specs for convection design

skill.name: transformation_ecosystem_architect
skill.coordination: Physical + digital + community integration for onboarding

skill.name: community_architect
skill.handoff: Ritual specs → platform setup + moderation guides

tier.name: Core
tier.day_1_variation: Standard welcome + How to Win + community intro

tier.name: Premium
tier.day_1_variation: Personalized welcome + live orientation call invite

tier.name: VIP
tier.day_1_variation: Personal call from team + custom onboarding path
tier.touch_frequency: Daily check-in first week, then as needed

dimensions.dimension: Retention trigger coverage (80%+ of required triggers)

track_patterns.pattern: Which Day 1 components have highest completion?
What breakthrough types create most sharing?
Which retention triggers have best re-engagement rates?
What intervention timing prevents most dropouts?
Which community rituals have highest participation?
What friction factors cause most support tickets?

refinement_process.step: Analyze completion patterns (where do people drop?)
Review intervention effectiveness (what brings people back?)
If metrics improve, apply patch and increment version

planned_improvements.improvement: Add cohort vs rolling enrollment variations

day_1.welcome: Personal video from sleep expert, "You made the right choice"
day_1.how_to_win: 3-minute guide: "The ONE thing that makes or breaks your success"
day_1.environment_setup: Sleep tracking app configured, bedroom environment checklist
day_1.first_do_task: Tonight's Sleep Hygiene Checklist (5 items, 10 minutes)

day_2.check_in: AI message: "How did last night go? Any issues with the checklist?"
day_2.friction_audit: Quick assessment: tech working? time blocked? space set up?
day_2.barrier_removal: Identify and fix one friction point
day_2.task: Continue sleep hygiene + add one new protocol element

day_3_5.methodology: Core sleep science + the "Sleep Stack" protocol
day_3_5.practice: Apply protocol nightly, track in app
day_3_5.community: Share Day 3 experience, ask one question
day_3_5.breakthrough_prep: Set baseline sleep score, target 20% improvement

day_6_7.breakthrough: 7th night with full protocol - measure sleep score
day_6_7.celebration: Post win in community with before/after scores
day_6_7.identity: You're now someone who prioritizes and achieves quality sleep
day_6_7.share: Optional: share on social with program hashtag

breakthrough_metric.measurement: Before/after comparison in app or journal

day_1.welcome: Live orientation call (or recording) + personal welcome message
day_1.how_to_win: "The 3 Mindsets of Our Most Successful Clients"
day_1.environment_setup: Calendar blocked, materials downloaded, Slack/community joined
day_1.first_do_task: Clarity Worksheet: 3 questions about current business + vision
day_1.community_intro: Intro thread with business description + 90-day goal

day_2.check_in: Email: "How did the Clarity Worksheet go? Reply with your biggest insight."
day_2.friction_audit: Tech check + calendar review + commitment confirmation
day_2.barrier_removal: Reschedule any conflicts with program time
day_2.task: Expand one answer from Clarity Worksheet into action plan

day_3_5.methodology: Core framework: Positioning + Offer Design basics
day_3_5.practice: Apply framework to their specific business
day_3_5.community: Share positioning statement for peer feedback
day_3_5.breakthrough_prep: Prepare for first outreach or offer test

day_6_7.breakthrough: First outreach batch (10 DMs) or offer draft complete
day_6_7.celebration: Post action taken + any early responses
day_6_7.identity: You're now someone who takes action on their business
day_6_7.share: LinkedIn post about committing to growth (optional)

breakthrough_metric.what: First business action (outreach, offer draft, or sales conversation)
breakthrough_metric.target: 10 outreach messages sent OR 1 complete offer draft
breakthrough_metric.measurement: Screenshot of sent messages or offer document

test.name: 7-Day Sequence Validation
test.input: Complete onboarding sequence with all components

test.name: Retention Trigger Coverage
test.input: Trigger config with scheduled, behavioral, achievement, social triggers

test.name: Dropout Prevention Readiness
test.input: Prevention config with signals, interventions, barrier types

test.name: Community Rituals Complete
test.input: Community config with welcome, win share, weekly, milestone rituals

test.name: Breakthrough Engineering Valid
test.input: Breakthrough config with metric, target, measurement, timeline

test.name: Low Friction Score
test.input: Onboarding design with optimized Day 1, simple setup, clear tasks

test.name: Escalation Path Complete
test.input: Support config with self-serve, community, coaching, 1:1 levels

quick_start.step: 1. Import transformation map from Block 1 (defines phases)
3. Define breakthrough metric (measurable Day 7-14 quick win)
4. Create retention triggers (scheduled + behavioral + achievement + social)
5. Build dropout prevention (warning signals + intervention protocol)

troubleshooting.solution: Reduce Day 1 length, simplify first task, add more check-ins Days 2-3. Friction is too high.
Breakthrough goal may be too ambitious. Reduce target to smallest measurable win.
Intro template may be missing. Add specific prompts. Have moderators welcome within 24 hours.
Day 1 is too long or complex. Simplify setup, shorten first task, make help more visible.
Post-breakthrough momentum lost. Add Week 2 preview on Day 7. Create next milestone immediately.
Reduce frequency, ensure every message has clear value, not just "check in."

faq.q: What if my product doesn't have a community?
faq.a: Community rituals become support rituals. Replace peer interaction with AI check-ins, email sequences, and direct support touchpoints.
faq.q: Can breakthrough take longer than 14 days?
faq.a: Yes, but label it properly. If breakthrough is Week 4, create a "mini-win" at Day 7 to maintain momentum.
faq.q: How do I know if my breakthrough is the right size?
faq.a: It should be: (1) Measurable, (2) Achievable in 7-14 days, (3) Meaningful enough to celebrate, (4) Connected to final transformation.
30-45 minutes maximum. Longer creates friction and reduces completion.
faq.q: Should I personalize onboarding by tier?
faq.a: Yes. Core = self-serve emphasis. Premium = add group coaching access. VIP = white-glove personal touch.
